metadata.title: Docker Security Hardening 2nd Pass — Enforce Seccomp, SOPS, MLflow Auth, Falco

setup.step: Verify you are NOT on fix/prefect-flow-connectivity or main
Read tdd-state.json — reset to new plan if it references docker-hardening-mlsecops-plan.xml

phase.name: Trivy CI Gate + Documentation
phase.description: Scripts and documentation additions only. No Dockerfile or compose file changes. All tests are unit-level (no Docker daemon required). Safest phase.

task.name: RED+GREEN: Trivy into pr_readiness_check.sh (#548)
task.description: Add Trivy CRITICAL scan as step [5/5] in scripts/pr_readiness_check.sh. Trivy images may not exist yet — skip gracefully if no minivess-* images are built. Also create scripts/weekly_security_scan.sh for scheduled scanning.
task.test-first:
Update tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_security_tooling.py:
test_pr_readiness_check_has_trivy_step(): content = Path("scripts/pr_readiness_check.sh").read_text(encoding="utf-8") lines = content.splitlines() # Find lines matching the step header pattern "\[N/N\]" (not substring-in-comment) step_lines = [l for l in lines if l.strip().startswith("[") and "/"] and "Trivy" in l] assert len(step_lines) >= 1, "No Trivy step found in pr_readiness_check.sh" # Verify it calls make trivy-critical trivy_lines = [l for l in lines if "trivy-critical" in l or "trivy_critical" in l] assert len(trivy_lines) >= 1
test_pr_readiness_check_trivy_skips_if_no_images(): content = Path("scripts/pr_readiness_check.sh").read_text(encoding="utf-8") # Must have a guard: skip if docker images | grep minivess returns empty assert "docker images" in content or "|| true" in content or "|| echo" in content, \ "Trivy step must skip gracefully if no images are built"
test_weekly_security_scan_exists_and_covers_all_flow_images(): script = Path("scripts/weekly_security_scan.sh") assert script.exists() content = script.read_text(encoding="utf-8") # Check image name prefixes (minivess-IMAGE, not service key strings) required_image_prefixes = [ "minivess-train", "minivess-data", "minivess-analyze", "minivess-hpo", "minivess-deploy", "minivess-dashboard", "minivess-acquisition", "minivess-annotation", "minivess-biostatistics", "minivess-post-training", "minivess-pipeline", "minivess-qa", ] for prefix in required_image_prefixes: assert prefix in content, f"weekly_security_scan.sh missing image: {prefix}"
task.implementation:
1. Edit scripts/pr_readiness_check.sh: - Change echo "=== ALL CHECKS PASSED ===" to step [5/5] - Add step: check if any minivess-* images exist; if yes, call make trivy-critical; if no images, print "[5/5] Trivy scan... SKIPPED (no images built yet)" and continue - Use: if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}" | grep -q "^minivess-"; then...
2. Create scripts/weekly_security_scan.sh: - Loops over all 12 image names (minivess-train, minivess-data, etc.) - Calls: trivy image --exit-code 0 --severity CRITICAL,HIGH "$image" || FAILURES+=("$image") - At end: if FAILURES non-empty, exit 1 with list of failed images - Header comment: "Run weekly: bash scripts/weekly_security_scan.sh" - Make executable: chmod +x
task.acceptance: - uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_security_tooling.py -k "trivy" passes - bash scripts/pr_readiness_check.sh runs without error (trivy step skips if no images) - bash -n scripts/weekly_security_scan.sh passes (syntax check)

task.name: RED+GREEN: Network isolation docs + rootless Docker note (#552, #549)
task.description: Add "Network Isolation Strategy" section to deployment/CLAUDE.md explaining why icc:false is banned. Add "Future Hardening / Upstream Blockers" section documenting rootless Docker blocker. Add ASCII network topology diagram. Create integration test scaffold (skips if Docker unavailable).
task.test-first:
Update tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_deployment_docs.py:
test_deployment_claude_md_has_network_isolation_section(): content = Path("deployment/CLAUDE.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8") assert "Network Isolation" in content assert "icc" in content and "false" in content # documents the ban assert "minivess-network" in content
test_deployment_claude_md_documents_rootless_docker_blocker(): content = Path("deployment/CLAUDE.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8") assert "rootless" in content.lower() assert "549" in content or "nvidia-container-toolkit" in content.lower()
test_deployment_claude_md_has_network_topology(): content = Path("deployment/CLAUDE.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8") # ASCII diagram must show key service relationships assert "minivess-mlflow" in content assert "minivess-prefect" in content or "prefect" in content.lower()
Create tests/integration/deployment/test_network_connectivity.py: - Add Docker availability guard at MODULE level (not just mark): try: subprocess.run(["docker", "info"], capture_output=True, check=True, timeout=5) DOCKER_AVAILABLE = True except (FileNotFoundError, subprocess.CalledProcessError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired): DOCKER_AVAILABLE = False
- All test functions: @pytest.mark.integration + skip if not DOCKER_AVAILABLE - test_flow_can_reach_mlflow: docker compose run curl -sf http://minivess-mlflow:5000/health - test_flow_can_reach_minio: docker compose run curl -sf http://minio:9000/minio/health/live - test_flow_can_reach_prefect: docker compose run curl -sf http://minivess-prefect:4200/api/health - Each test uses --rm --no-deps and passes --env-file (deployment/CLAUDE.md pattern)
task.implementation:
1. Edit deployment/CLAUDE.md — add after existing content:
## Network Isolation Strategy
All MinIVess services share `minivess-network` (custom user-defined bridge). Flow containers reach infra via service names: `minivess-mlflow`, `minio`, `minivess-prefect`.
ASCII topology: ``` [train/hpo/analyze] ──► [minivess-mlflow:5000] (MLflow tracking) ──► [minio:9000] (artifact store) ──► [minivess-prefect:4200] (orchestration) [dashboard/qa] ──► [minivess-mlflow:5000] (read-only queries) All services share: minivess-network (external, pre-created) ```
### Why `icc: false` Is BANNED
`icc: false` in `/etc/docker/daemon.json` is a CIS Benchmark 2.1 recommendation but is INCOMPATIBLE with this project. Reason: - `icc: false` affects the default bridge only — but some Docker Compose versions apply it to ALL networks including `minivess-network` - Breaking it causes: MLflow 403/connection refused, MinIO artifact upload failures, Prefect heartbeat timeouts — all silent failures that look like training bugs - Correct isolation: explicit `networks:` declaration per service (already done)
### Future: Rootless Docker (Blocked — Issue #549)
Rootless Docker (daemon without root socket) is blocked by NVIDIA Container Toolkit issue #434 on Ubuntu 24.04. The CDI spec generator does not produce correct rootless specs. Monitor https://github.com/NVIDIA/nvidia-container-toolkit/releases. DO NOT attempt workarounds — they break GPU access.
2. Create tests/integration/deployment/__init__.py (empty) 3. Create tests/integration/deployment/test_network_connectivity.py (as spec'd above)
task.acceptance: - uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_deployment_docs.py passes - tests/integration/deployment/test_network_connectivity.py is collected without error (all 3 tests skip if Docker unavailable — verified with pytest --collect-only)

phase.name: Seccomp Enforce Mode
phase.description: Build train-enforce.json from the Docker moby default seccomp profile (well-maintained ~300-syscall allow-list for container workloads) extended with GPU/ML syscalls. Wire GPU flow services in docker-compose.flows.yml to use the enforce profile. Uses ABSOLUTE path from REPO_ROOT to avoid CWD-relative resolution bugs. Does NOT require a live training run to collect syscalls.

task.name: RED: seccomp enforce profile tests (#547)
task.description: Write tests that will fail until train-enforce.json is created and wired into compose. Tests verify: file exists, defaultAction=SCMP_ACT_ERRNO, syscalls list is non-empty, no duplicates, train-enforce is a strict superset of audit.json syscalls.
task.test-first:
Create tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_seccomp_enforce.py:
import json from pathlib import Path
SECCOMP_DIR = Path("deployment/seccomp") AUDIT_PROFILE = SECCOMP_DIR / "audit.json" ENFORCE_PROFILE = SECCOMP_DIR / "train-enforce.json"
def _load_profile(path: Path) -> dict: return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
def test_train_enforce_profile_exists(): assert ENFORCE_PROFILE.exists(), f"Missing: {ENFORCE_PROFILE}"
def test_train_enforce_default_action_is_errno(): profile = _load_profile(ENFORCE_PROFILE) assert profile["defaultAction"] == "SCMP_ACT_ERRNO", \ f"Expected SCMP_ACT_ERRNO, got {profile['defaultAction']}"
def test_train_enforce_has_nonempty_syscall_allow_list(): profile = _load_profile(ENFORCE_PROFILE) syscalls = profile.get("syscalls", []) allow_entries = [s for s in syscalls if s.get("action") == "SCMP_ACT_ALLOW"] names = [] for entry in allow_entries: names.extend(entry.get("names", [])) assert len(names) >= 200, f"Expected >=200 allowed syscalls, got {len(names)}" # No empty strings in names assert all(n for n in names), "Found empty syscall name in allow list"
def test_train_enforce_has_no_duplicate_syscall_names(): profile = _load_profile(ENFORCE_PROFILE) all_names = [] for entry in profile.get("syscalls", []): all_names.extend(entry.get("names", [])) duplicates = [n for n in set(all_names) if all_names.count(n) > 1] assert not duplicates, f"Duplicate syscall names: {duplicates}"
def test_train_enforce_is_superset_of_audit_syscalls(): """audit.json has empty syscalls (SCMP_ACT_LOG=allow-all). Enforce must allow at least the Docker default set. Since audit has no explicit names, we verify enforce has >= 200 names (Docker default has ~305). This is the superset invariant.""" enforce = _load_profile(ENFORCE_PROFILE) all_names = [] for entry in enforce.get("syscalls", []): all_names.extend(entry.get("names", [])) assert len(all_names) >= 200, \ "train-enforce.json must include Docker-default syscall set (>=200 names)"
def test_train_enforce_includes_gpu_specific_syscalls(): profile = _load_profile(ENFORCE_PROFILE) all_names = [] for entry in profile.get("syscalls", []): all_names.extend(entry.get("names", [])) # GPU/ML specific additions beyond Docker default gpu_syscalls = ["perf_event_open", "get_mempolicy", "mbind", "set_mempolicy"] for sc in gpu_syscalls: assert sc in all_names, f"GPU syscall '{sc}' missing from enforce profile"
def test_compose_gpu_services_reference_enforce_profile(): import yaml compose = yaml.safe_load( Path("deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml").read_text(encoding="utf-8") ) gpu_services = ["train", "hpo", "hpo-worker"] for svc in gpu_services: service_def = compose["services"][svc] security_opts = service_def.get("security_opt", []) seccomp_opts = [o for o in security_opts if "seccomp" in o] assert seccomp_opts, f"Service '{svc}' missing seccomp security_opt" assert "train-enforce.json" in seccomp_opts[0], \ f"Service '{svc}' should reference train-enforce.json"
task.implementation: None yet — this is the RED phase. All tests should FAIL.
task.acceptance: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_seccomp_enforce.py — all tests FAIL (confirms the tests are real and detecting the missing implementation)

task.name: GREEN: Create train-enforce.json + wire compose (#547)
task.description:
Build train-enforce.json from the Docker moby default seccomp profile (https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/master/profiles/seccomp/default.json) plus GPU/ML-specific syscalls. Wire GPU compose services to use it.
CRITICAL: seccomp path in docker-compose.flows.yml must resolve from REPO_ROOT. Use: deployment/seccomp/train-enforce.json (relative to repo root, always invoked from repo root via scripts/run_debug.sh). Document in deployment/CLAUDE.md.
CRITICAL: Do NOT use --users-db-uri (not a valid MLflow flag — reserved for Phase 4).
task.implementation:
1. Create deployment/seccomp/train-enforce.json: Structure: { "defaultAction": "SCMP_ACT_ERRNO", "architectures": ["SCMP_ARCH_X86_64"], "syscalls": [ { "names": [ ... (Docker moby default allow-list: ~305 syscalls including accept4, access, adjtimex, alarm, bind, brk, capget, capset, chdir, chmod, chown, clock_getres, clock_gettime, clock_nanosleep, close, connect, copy_file_range, creat, dup, dup2, dup3, epoll_create, epoll_create1, epoll_ctl, epoll_pwait, epoll_wait, eventfd, eventfd2, execve, execveat, exit, exit_group, faccessat, faccessat2, fadvise64, fallocate, fanotify_mark, fchdir, fchmod, fchmodat, fchown, fchownat, fcntl, fdatasync, fgetxattr, flistxattr, flock, fork, fremovexattr, fsetxattr, fstat, fstatfs, fsync, ftruncate, futex, getcpu, getcwd, getdents, getdents64, getegid, geteuid, getgid, getgroups, getitimer, getpeername, getpgid, getpgrp, getpid, getppid, getpriority, getrandom, getresgid, getresuid, getrlimit, getrusage, getsid, getsockname, getsockopt, gettid, gettimeofday, getuid, getxattr, inotify_add_watch, inotify_init, inotify_init1, inotify_rm_watch, ioctl, ioprio_get, ioprio_set, ipc, kill, lchown, lgetxattr, link, linkat, listen, listxattr, llistxattr, lremovexattr, lseek, lsetxattr, lstat, madvise, memfd_create, mincore, mkdir, mkdirat, mknod, mknodat, mlock, mlock2, mlockall, mmap, mprotect, mq_getsetattr, mq_notify, mq_open, mq_timedreceive, mq_timedsend, mq_unlink, mremap, msgctl, msgget, msgrcv, msgsnd, msync, munlock, munlockall, munmap, nanosleep, newfstatat, open, openat, openat2, pause, personality, pipe, pipe2, poll, ppoll, prctl, pread64, preadv, preadv2, prlimit64, pselect6, ptrace, pwrite64, pwritev, pwritev2, read, readahead, readlink, readlinkat, readv, recv, recvfrom, recvmmsg, recvmsg, remap_file_pages, removexattr, rename, renameat, renameat2, restart_syscall, rmdir, rt_sigaction, rt_sigpending, rt_sigprocmask, rt_sigqueueinfo, rt_sigreturn, rt_sigsuspend, rt_sigtimedwait, rt_tgsigqueueinfo, sched_getaffinity, sched_getattr, sched_getparam, sched_getscheduler, sched_get_priority_max, sched_get_priority_min, sched_rr_get_interval, sched_setaffinity, sched_setattr, sched_setparam, sched_setscheduler, sched_yield, seccomp, select, semctl, semget, semop, semtimedop, send, sendfile, sendmmsg, sendmsg, sendto, setfsgid, setfsuid, setgid, setgroups, setitimer, setpgid, setpriority, setregid, setresgid, setresuid, setreuid, setrlimit, setsid, setsockopt, setuid, setxattr, shmat, shmctl, shmdt, shmget, shutdown, sigaltstack, signalfd, signalfd4, sigreturn, socket, socketpair, splice, stat, statfs, statx, symlink, symlinkat, sync, sync_file_range, syncfs, sysinfo, syslog, tee, tgkill, time, timer_create, timer_delete, timer_getoverrun, timer_gettime, timer_settime, timerfd_create, timerfd_gettime, timerfd_settime, times, tkill, truncate, uname, unlink, unlinkat, unshare, utime, utimensat, utimes, vfork, vmsplice, wait4, waitid, write, writev) ], "action": "SCMP_ACT_ALLOW" }, { "names": ["perf_event_open", "get_mempolicy", "mbind", "set_mempolicy"], "action": "SCMP_ACT_ALLOW", "comment": "GPU/NUMA memory policy syscalls for CUDA/PyTorch training" } ] }
NOTE: The exact syscall list must come from the Docker moby default profile. Use WebFetch to retrieve the latest from: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/moby/moby/master/profiles/seccomp/default.json and extract the names arrays. This ensures the list is maintained by Docker.
2. Edit deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml: Add x-gpu-security YAML anchor after x-common-env: x-gpu-security: &gpu-security security_opt: - "seccomp=deployment/seccomp/train-enforce.json" cap_drop: [ALL] no_new_privileges: true
For train, hpo, hpo-worker services: replace cap_drop/no_new_privileges with <<: *gpu-security (merge the anchor). Keep shm_size and resource limits as-is.
NOTE: YAML merge does not deep-merge lists. The anchor must contain cap_drop and no_new_privileges to avoid divergence. Add comment: # x-gpu-security MUST keep cap_drop+no_new_privileges in sync with x-common-security. # Run: grep -n 'cap_drop\|no_new_privileges' deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml # to verify all GPU services use gpu-security anchor.
3. Update deployment/seccomp/README.md: - Add: "Current status: train-enforce.json deployed (SCMP_ACT_ERRNO)" - Add note: "seccomp path is relative to REPO_ROOT. Always invoke docker compose from the repo root or use scripts/run_debug.sh (which does this automatically)."
task.acceptance: - deployment/seccomp/train-enforce.json exists, parses as valid JSON - uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_seccomp_enforce.py — all 6 tests PASS - uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_compose_hardening.py — still PASS (regression: cap_drop not broken for GPU services) - bash -n deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml fails gracefully (YAML valid) - docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml config validates

phase.name: SOPS Scaffolding and Credential Rotation
phase.description:
Scaffold SOPS+age support for team deployments WITHOUT encrypting the user's .env. Deliverables: .sops.yaml config, scripts/setup_dev.sh, scripts/_rotate_credentials.py (Python, Rule #16 compliant — uses str.split, not sed/regex).
CRITICAL Rule #16: init_credentials uses Python str.split("=", 1) to parse .env, NOT sed with regex patterns. Shell script calls `uv run python scripts/_rotate_credentials.py`.

task.name: RED: SOPS scaffolding tests (#550, #538)
task.description: Write failing tests for SOPS config, setup_dev.sh, and credential rotation.
task.implementation: None yet — RED phase. Tests should FAIL.
task.acceptance: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_secrets_management.py — all tests FAIL

task.name: GREEN: SOPS config + setup_dev.sh + credential rotation (#550, #538)
task.description: Create all SOPS scaffolding files. Python credential rotation (Rule #16).
task.implementation:
1. Create .sops.yaml: creation_rules: - path_regex: '\.env(\.enc)?$' key_groups: - age: - "age1REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_AGE_PUBLIC_KEY" # Generate your age key: age-keygen -o ~/.config/sops/age/keys.txt # Get public key: age-keygen -y ~/.config/sops/age/keys.txt - path_regex: '.*' key_groups: - age: - "age1REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_AGE_PUBLIC_KEY"
2. Create scripts/setup_dev.sh: #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail # MinIVess dev environment setup. # Run once after cloning or when .env.enc changes. REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)" ENV_FILE="$REPO_ROOT/.env" ENC_FILE="$REPO_ROOT/.env.enc" if [[ -f "$ENC_FILE" ]]; then echo "[setup_dev] Decrypting .env.enc → .env (requires age key in ~/.config/sops/age/keys.txt)" sops --decrypt "$ENC_FILE" > "$ENV_FILE" echo "[setup_dev] .env decrypted successfully" elif [[ -f "$ENV_FILE" ]]; then echo "[setup_dev] .env already present (no .env.enc — using as-is)" else echo "[setup_dev] ERROR: neither .env nor .env.enc found" echo " Copy .env.example → .env and fill in your values:" echo " cp .env.example .env" exit 1 fi # Validate required vars are non-empty required_vars=("MODEL_CACHE_HOST_PATH" "MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD" "POSTGRES_PASSWORD") for var in "${required_vars[@]}"; do val=$(grep "^${var}=" "$ENV_FILE" | cut -d= -f2-) if [[ -z "$val" ]] || [[ "$val" == *"REPLACE"* ]]; then echo "[setup_dev] WARNING: $var is unset or still a placeholder in .env" fi done echo "[setup_dev] Environment ready. Run: docker compose --env-file .env -f deployment/docker-compose.yml up -d"
3. Create scripts/_rotate_credentials.py: """ Credential rotation utility for MinIVess MLOps. Generates new random credentials and writes them to .env. Rule #16 compliant: uses str.split("=", 1), NOT regex or sed. """ from __future__ import annotations import secrets import sys from pathlib import Path
REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent ENV_FILE = REPO_ROOT / ".env"
# Variables to rotate with secrets.token_hex(32) ROTATE_TARGETS = [ "POSTGRES_PASSWORD", "MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD", "LANGFUSE_SECRET", "LANGFUSE_SALT", "GRAFANA_PASSWORD", ]
def rotate(env_file: Path = ENV_FILE) -> None: if not env_file.exists(): print(f"ERROR: {env_file} not found. Run: cp .env.example .env") sys.exit(1) lines = env_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines() new_lines = [] rotated = [] for line in lines: if "=" in line and not line.startswith("#"): key, _, value = line.partition("=") key = key.strip() if key in ROTATE_TARGETS: new_value = secrets.token_hex(32) new_lines.append(f"{key}={new_value}") rotated.append(key) continue new_lines.append(line) env_file.write_text("\n".join(new_lines) + "\n", encoding="utf-8") print(f"Rotated {len(rotated)} credentials: {', '.join(rotated)}") print("WARNING: Update OPTUNA_STORAGE_URL in .env to match new POSTGRES_PASSWORD") print("NEXT: docker compose --env-file .env restart")
if __name__ == "__main__": rotate()
4. Update .env.example — add SOPS section header before first variable group: # Team Setup (multi-developer): # Install age: sudo apt-get install age # Install sops: see https://github.com/getsops/sops/releases # Decrypt env: bash scripts/setup_dev.sh # Rotate credentials: uv run python scripts/_rotate_credentials.py
5. chmod +x scripts/setup_dev.sh
task.acceptance: - uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_secrets_management.py — all PASS - bash -n scripts/setup_dev.sh (syntax check passes) - uv run python scripts/_rotate_credentials.py --help or dry-run on a temp .env - uv run ruff check scripts/_rotate_credentials.py - uv run mypy scripts/_rotate_credentials.py

phase.name: MLflow Auth Opt-In
phase.description:
Add MLflow basic-auth as an opt-in docker compose profile "secure". Default workflow (solo dev) remains unchanged — mlflow service has no auth. The "secure" profile adds mlflow-secure service on the SAME PORT — both cannot run simultaneously. Tests verify this mutual exclusivity is documented.
CRITICAL: MLflow basic-auth does NOT use --users-db-uri (not a valid MLflow 3.x flag). Use --app-name basic-auth. MLflow auto-creates basic_auth.db in working directory. auth.ini is added to .gitignore (it may contain hashed passwords). New env vars go in .env.example first (Rule #22) and are tested by test_env_single_source.py.

task.name: RED: MLflow auth tests (#553)
task.description: Write failing tests for auth config, env vars, tracking.py credential support.
task.test-first:
Create tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_mlflow_auth.py:
import yaml from pathlib import Path
def test_mlflow_auth_ini_example_exists(): assert Path("deployment/mlflow/auth.ini.example").exists()
def test_mlflow_auth_ini_example_has_required_sections(): content = Path("deployment/mlflow/auth.ini.example").read_text(encoding="utf-8") assert "[mlflow]" in content assert "default_permission" in content assert "admin_username" in content or "username" in content
def test_mlflow_auth_ini_is_gitignored(): gitignore = Path(".gitignore").read_text(encoding="utf-8") gitignore_lines = gitignore.splitlines() # auth.ini (with real passwords) must be gitignored assert any("auth.ini" in line and not line.startswith("#") for line in gitignore_lines), \ "deployment/mlflow/auth.ini must be in .gitignore"
def test_env_example_has_mlflow_auth_vars(): content = Path(".env.example").read_text(encoding="utf-8") assert "MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME" in content assert "MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD" in content
def test_init_mlflow_auth_script_exists(): assert Path("scripts/init_mlflow_auth.sh").exists() content = Path("scripts/init_mlflow_auth.sh").read_text(encoding="utf-8") # Must call mlflow server --app-name basic-auth (not --users-db-uri) assert "basic-auth" in content assert "users-db-uri" not in content, \ "--users-db-uri is not a valid MLflow 3.x flag"
def test_compose_has_mlflow_secure_profile(): compose = yaml.safe_load( Path("deployment/docker-compose.yml").read_text(encoding="utf-8") ) services = compose.get("services", {}) assert "mlflow-secure" in services, "mlflow-secure service missing" secure = services["mlflow-secure"] profiles = secure.get("profiles", []) assert "secure" in profiles, "mlflow-secure must be under 'secure' profile" # Verify --app-name basic-auth in command command = str(secure.get("command", "")) assert "basic-auth" in command
def test_mlflow_and_mlflow_secure_cannot_share_port_simultaneously(): """Both bind the same port — document they are mutually exclusive.""" content = Path("deployment/docker-compose.yml").read_text(encoding="utf-8") assert "mutually exclusive" in content.lower() or \ "cannot run simultaneously" in content.lower() or \ "stop mlflow before" in content.lower(), \ "docker-compose.yml must document that mlflow and mlflow-secure are mutually exclusive"
Update tests/v2/unit/test_env_single_source.py to verify no duplicate definitions of MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME / MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD in Dynaconf TOMLs. (Follow existing patterns in that test file.)
task.acceptance: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_mlflow_auth.py — all tests FAIL (RED confirmed)

task.name: GREEN: MLflow auth files + compose profile + tracking.py (#553)
task.description: Create auth config files, add mlflow-secure compose profile, update tracking.py. Keep default mlflow service unchanged (no-auth remains default for solo dev).
task.implementation:
1. Create deployment/mlflow/ directory.
2. Create deployment/mlflow/auth.ini.example: [mlflow] default_permission = READ database_uri = sqlite:///basic_auth.db admin_username = admin admin_password = REPLACE_WITH_STRONG_PASSWORD # Generate password: uv run python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(32))"
3. Add to .gitignore: deployment/mlflow/auth.ini
4. Add to .env.example (per Rule #22 — single source of truth): # ─── MLflow Auth (optional — activate with docker compose --profile secure) ──── MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME= MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD=
5. Edit deployment/docker-compose.yml — add mlflow-secure service after mlflow: # mlflow-secure: MLflow with basic-auth. Profile: "secure". # MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with default mlflow service — both bind MLFLOW_PORT. # Cannot run simultaneously. Stop mlflow before starting mlflow-secure: # docker compose stop mlflow # docker compose --profile secure up mlflow-secure mlflow-secure: build: context: .. dockerfile: deployment/docker/Dockerfile.mlflow image: minivess-mlflow:v3.10.0 container_name: minivess-mlflow profiles: ["secure"] environment: MLFLOW_AUTH_CONFIG_PATH: /etc/mlflow/auth.ini command: >- mlflow server --backend-store-uri postgresql://${POSTGRES_USER:-minivess}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-minivess_secret}@${POSTGRES_DOCKER_HOST:-postgres}:5432/${POSTGRES_DB_MLFLOW:-mlflow} --artifacts-destination s3://${MLFLOW_ARTIFACT_BUCKET:-mlflow-artifacts} --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${MLFLOW_PORT:-5000} --app-name basic-auth volumes: - ./mlflow/auth.ini:/etc/mlflow/auth.ini:ro ... (same depends_on, networks, ports as default mlflow service)
6. Create scripts/init_mlflow_auth.sh: #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail # Initialise MLflow basic-auth. Run once after deploying mlflow-secure. # Creates admin user and sets default permissions. MLFLOW_URI="${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI:-http://localhost:5000}" echo "Initialising MLflow auth at $MLFLOW_URI" echo "After running: set MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME and MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD in .env" # MLflow basic-auth creates basic_auth.db automatically on first --app-name basic-auth start. # This script documents the post-startup steps: echo "Step 1: Start mlflow-secure: docker compose --profile secure up mlflow-secure" echo "Step 2: Set env vars in .env: MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME=admin MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD=..." echo "Step 3: Test: curl -u admin:password http://localhost:5000/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/list"
7. Edit src/minivess/observability/tracking.py — update resolve_tracking_uri(): After existing URI resolution, check for auth credentials: username = os.environ.get("MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME") password = os.environ.get("MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD") if username and password: # Inject credentials into URI: http://user:pass@host:port from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlunparse parsed = urlparse(tracking_uri) authed = parsed._replace(netloc=f"{username}:{password}@{parsed.netloc}") tracking_uri = urlunparse(authed) return tracking_uri
task.acceptance: - uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_mlflow_auth.py — all PASS - uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/test_env_single_source.py — still PASS - uv run mypy src/minivess/observability/tracking.py — clean - uv run ruff check src/minivess/observability/tracking.py - docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.yml config validates (YAML valid)

phase.name: Falco Runtime Monitoring
phase.description: Add Falco as an optional Docker Compose profile "security". Falco requires privileged: true — regression test guards other services remain unprivileged after this change. Lightweight: config files + compose service definition only. No Kubernetes/Helm — local Docker Compose only.

task.name: RED: Falco tests (#551)
task.description: Write failing tests for Falco rules YAML and compose service.
task.acceptance: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_falco.py — all FAIL (RED confirmed)

task.name: GREEN: Falco rules + compose service (#551)
task.description: Create ML-specific Falco rules targeting model weight exfiltration, shell spawns, and unexpected outbound connections. Add falco service to docker-compose.yml under profile "security". Falco requires privileged: true (documented + tested).
task.implementation:
1. Create deployment/falco/ directory.
2. Create deployment/falco/minivess_rules.yaml: # MinIVess MLOps — Custom Falco Security Rules # These rules detect anomalous behaviour specific to ML training containers. # Run Falco: docker compose --profile security up falco # Reference: https://falco.org/docs/rules/
- rule: MinIVess checkpoint exfiltration desc: A process other than the training script writes checkpoints to a remote destination condition: > container.name startswith "minivess-flow" and (evt.type = connect or evt.type = sendto) and fd.name contains "/app/checkpoints" output: > Possible model exfiltration (user=%user.name command=%proc.cmdline container=%container.name file=%fd.name) priority: WARNING tags: [minivess, ml-security, exfiltration]
- rule: MinIVess shell spawn in flow container desc: A shell process is spawned inside a running flow container (deserialization attack) condition: > container.name startswith "minivess-flow" and evt.type = execve and (proc.name = bash or proc.name = sh or proc.name = dash) and proc.pname != "run_debug.sh" and proc.pname != "entrypoint.sh" output: > Shell spawned in flow container (user=%user.name shell=%proc.name parent=%proc.pname container=%container.name) priority: CRITICAL tags: [minivess, ml-security, shell-spawn]
- rule: MinIVess unexpected outbound connection desc: Flow container connects to an IP not in the expected service list condition: > container.name startswith "minivess-flow" and evt.type = connect and fd.typechar = 4 and not fd.rip in (minivess-mlflow, minio, minivess-prefect, postgres) output: > Unexpected outbound connection from flow container (container=%container.name dest=%fd.rip:%fd.rport command=%proc.cmdline) priority: WARNING tags: [minivess, ml-security, network]
3. Edit deployment/docker-compose.yml — add falco service: # Falco runtime security monitoring. Optional — activate with: # docker compose --profile security up falco # Falco REQUIRES privileged: true for eBPF kernel module access. # All other services remain unprivileged (tested: test_falco.py). falco: image: falcosecurity/falco-no-driver:latest container_name: minivess-falco profiles: ["security"] privileged: true volumes: - /var/run/docker.sock:/host/var/run/docker.sock:ro - /proc:/host/proc:ro - ./falco/minivess_rules.yaml:/etc/falco/rules.d/minivess.yaml:ro environment: FALCO_BPF_PROBE: "" command: ["/usr/bin/falco", "--modern-bpf", "-r", "/etc/falco/rules.d/minivess.yaml"] networks: - minivess restart: "no"
4. Update deployment/CLAUDE.md — add Falco section: ## Falco Runtime Security Monitoring (Optional)
Falco detects anomalous container behaviour in real-time. Activate with: ```bash docker compose --profile security up falco ``` Custom rules: `deployment/falco/minivess_rules.yaml` Alerts on: model weight exfiltration, shell spawns, unexpected network connections.
NOTE: Falco requires `privileged: true` for eBPF kernel access. All other MinIVess services remain unprivileged (enforced by test_falco.py).
task.acceptance: - uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_falco.py — all PASS - uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_compose_hardening.py — still PASS (regression: non-Falco services not privileged) - docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.yml config validates - yamllint deployment/falco/minivess_rules.yaml (valid YAML)

task.name: VERIFY: Full test suite + commit + push
task.description: Run all deployment tests, check for regressions, commit, push, and update tdd-state.json.
task.implementation: 1. uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/ -v --tb=short 2. uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/test_env_single_source.py 3. uv run ruff check src/minivess/ tests/ 4. uv run mypy src/minivess/observability/tracking.py 5. docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.yml config (YAML validates) 6. docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml config (YAML validates) 7. git add -p (review each change before staging) 8. git commit 9. git push origin feat/docker-hardening-2nd-pass 10. Update tdd-state.json: all tasks DONE, convergence.reached = true
task.acceptance: - All tests pass (no regressions in test_compose_hardening.py, test_security_tooling.py, test_deployment_docs.py, test_env_single_source.py) - ruff: no errors - mypy: no new errors - Both compose files validate - Closes issues: #547, #548, #549, #550, #551, #552, #553
